License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.KRaftVersionRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.LeaderChangeMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotFooterRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotHeaderRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.VotersRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetention;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetentionResult;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Objects;\n\n/**\n * In-memory implementation of {@link Records} backed by a ByteBuffer.\n *\n * <p><b>Primary Use Cases:</b>\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Reading or modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches from the log or network.</li>\n *   <li>Iterating over batches and records for filtering, validation, or processing.</li>\n * </ul>\n * To create new records from scratch, use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} or factory methods like\n * {@link #withRecords(Compression, SimpleRecord...)}, {@link #withIdempotentRecords}, etc.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> This class is <b>not thread-safe</b>. The backing ByteBuffer and batch\n * objects can be modified in-place (e.g., CRC updates during log appending). External synchronization\n * is required for concurrent access. However, individual batch iteration is safe if the buffer contents\n * are not modified during iteration.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b>\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Batch iteration is lazy—batches are parsed incrementally, not all at once.</li>\n *   <li>Record decompression is lazy—only decompressed when actually read via iterators.</li>\n *   <li>No copying of record data; all reads directly reference the backing buffer via slices.</li>\n *   <li>{@link #validBytes()} requires scanning all batches; the result is cached.</li>\n * </ul></p>\n *\n * <p><b>Buffer Management:</b> The ByteBuffer backing this instance can be in several states:\n * <ul>\n *   <li><b>Read-only:</b> Created via {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)}, position/limit managed externally.</li>\n *   <li><b>Writable:</b> Created via {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder#build()}, position and limit reflect valid data.</li>\n * </ul></p>\n *\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for creating new record batches\n * @see RecordBatch for batch-level operations\n * @see Record for record-level operations\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n     * @param channel The channel to write to\n     * @return The number of bytes written\n     * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * The total number of bytes in this record collection that form complete, valid batches.\n     *\n     * <p>This may be smaller than {@link #sizeInBytes()} if the buffer contains incomplete or\n     * trailing partial batches (e.g., during network reception). Incomplete batches are excluded\n     * from this count but present in the buffer.</p>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance:</b> The first call requires scanning all batches to compute the total;\n     * the result is cached for subsequent calls. If the batch iteration is expensive (e.g., large\n     * record sets with many batches), consider caching the result at a higher level.</p>\n     *\n     * @return the number of bytes in complete, valid batches\n     *\n     * @see #sizeInBytes() for the total buffer size (including partial batches)\n     * @see #batches() to iterate over individual batches\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {\n        if (validBytes >= 0)\n            return validBytes;\n\n        int bytes = 0;\n        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n        this.validBytes = bytes;\n        return bytes;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\n     * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\n     *         magic byte, null otherwise\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\n     */\n    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n            return null;\n        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filter records based on retention rules, writing results to a destination buffer.\n     *\n     * <p>This method is used for log compaction and record expiration, applying batch and record-level\n     * retention decisions via the provided {@code filter}. Batches can be entirely deleted or retained\n     * (possibly with records removed). Empty batches may be retained to preserve producer state for\n     * idempotent/transactional producers.</p>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Filtering Process:</b>\n     * <ol>\n     *   <li>Each batch is evaluated via {@link RecordFilter#checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}.</li>\n     *   <li>If batch retention is {@link RecordFilter.BatchRetention#DELETE}, the entire batch is skipped.</li>\n     *   <li>Otherwise, each record is evaluated via {@link RecordFilter#shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)}.</li>\n     *   <li>Retained records are written to {@code destinationBuffer}, possibly in a new batch if\n     *       modification was necessary.</li>\n     * </ol></p>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance:</b> For compressed batches, this method decompresses records on-demand using\n     * the supplied {@code decompressionBufferSupplier}. Reusable buffer suppliers significantly improve\n     * performance for small batches where allocation dominates. Buffer overflow is handled transparently\n     * by allocating larger buffers as needed.</p>\n     *\n     * @param filter filter defining batch and record retention logic\n     * @param destinationBuffer buffer to write filtered records to. If overflow occurs, a larger buffer\n     *        is allocated and returned in {@code FilterResult#outputBuffer()}\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier supplier for decompression buffers. For performance with small\n     *        batches, use {@link BufferSupplier#create()} which reuses buffers within the lifetime\n     *        of the returned resource.\n     *\n     * @return a {@link FilterResult} containing the filtered records buffer, metrics (messages read/retained,\n     *         bytes read/retained), and max offset/timestamp information\n     *\n     * @see RecordFilter for batch and record retention semantics\n     * @see FilterResult for the structure of returned filter metrics\n     */\n    public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n     * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n     */\n    private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n                                         ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n        for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n            final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n            final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n\n            filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n            if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n                continue;\n\n            final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n                filter);\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n            boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n            long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n\n            if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n                // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n                // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n                // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n                boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n                    batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n                if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n                    batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n                    filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n                } else {\n                    long deleteHorizonMs;\n                    if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n                        deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n                    else\n                        deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n                    try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n                            bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n                        MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n                        int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n                        MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n                        filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n                            maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n                if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n\n                bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n                DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n                        batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n                        batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n                        batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n                filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n            }\n\n            // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n            // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n            ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n            if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n                filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n                return filterResult;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return filterResult;\n    }\n\n    private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n                                                 BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n                                                 FilterResult filterResult,\n                                                 RecordFilter filter) {\n        try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n            long maxOffset = -1;\n            boolean containsTombstones = false;\n            // Convert records with old record versions\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iterator.next();\n                filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n\n                if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n                    if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n                        maxOffset = record.offset();\n\n                    retainedRecords.add(record);\n\n                    if (!record.hasValue()) {\n                        containsTombstones = true;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    writeOriginalBatch = false;\n                }\n            }\n            return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class BatchFilterResult {\n        private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n        private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n        private final boolean containsTombstones;\n        private final long maxOffset;\n        private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                  final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n                                  final boolean containsTombstones,\n                                  final long maxOffset) {\n            this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n            this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n            this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n            this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n                                                                 List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                                                 ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n                                                                 final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n        Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n        // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n        // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n        // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n        // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n        TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n        long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n                originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n                originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n\n        // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n        MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n                compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n                originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n                originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n\n        for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n            builder.append(record);\n\n        if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n            // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n            // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n            // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n            builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n\n        return builder;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n     */\n    public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n        return buffer.duplicate();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n        if (position < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n        if (position > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n        if (size < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n\n        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n        slicedBuffer.position(position);\n        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n\n        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n        return batches;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n                \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n                \")\";\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n\n        return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer.hashCode();\n    }\n\n    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        public final long currentTime;\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }\n\n        public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n            public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n            public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n                                        final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n                this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n                this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public enum BatchRetention {\n            DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n            RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n            DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n         * check the records individually).\n         */\n        protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n         * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n         * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n         */\n        protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n    }\n\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n            int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\n            updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\n                    retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset,\n                                                 int messagesRetained, int bytesRetained) {\n            validateBatchMetadata(maxTimestamp, shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\n            if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n                this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n                this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n            }\n            this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n            this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n            this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\n            if (maxTimestamp != RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP && shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"shallowOffset undefined for maximum timestamp \" + maxTimestamp);\n            if (maxOffset < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"maxOffset undefined\");\n        }\n\n        public ByteBuffer outputBuffer() {\n            return outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRead() {\n            return messagesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRead() {\n            return bytesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRetained() {\n            return messagesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRetained() {\n            return bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public long maxOffset() {\n            return maxOffset;\n        }\n\n        public long maxTimestamp() {\n            return maxTimestamp;\n        }\n\n        public long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n            return shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a {@code MemoryRecords} instance for reading existing record batches from a buffer.\n     *\n     * <p>The returned instance provides read-only access to record batches. The buffer is read from\n     * its current position to its limit. For batch iteration, the buffer position is not modified;\n     * a duplicate buffer is used internally to preserve the original state.</p>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Use Cases:</b> Reading records from the log, network buffer, or in-memory batch collections.\n     * Use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} or {@link #withRecords} variants to create new records.</p>\n     *\n     * @param buffer ByteBuffer containing serialized record batches. The buffer's position and limit\n     *        define the readable range. The buffer must remain valid for the lifetime of the returned\n     *        MemoryRecords instance.\n     *\n     * @return a MemoryRecords instance wrapping the buffer for reading batches\n     *\n     * @throws NullPointerException if buffer is null\n     *\n     * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder to create new records\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a builder for constructing new record batches using the current record format version.\n     *\n     * <p>This convenience method uses {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} (magic v2). For legacy\n     * format support, use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)}.</p>\n     *\n     * <p>The builder initializes batches with:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>No producer ID (default non-idempotent producer)</li>\n     *   <li>No transaction markers</li>\n     *   <li>Current system time for {@code LOG_APPEND_TIME} timestamp type</li>\n     * </ul></p>\n     *\n     * @param buffer ByteBuffer to write batches to. Must have sufficient remaining capacity for\n     *        the records to be added. Can be passed to subsequent {@code builder()} calls to\n     *        accumulate multiple batches.\n     * @param compression compression type for the records\n     * @param timestampType whether timestamps are CREATE_TIME (client) or LOG_APPEND_TIME (broker)\n     * @param baseOffset offset of the first record in the batch\n     *\n     * @return a MemoryRecordsBuilder ready to append records\n     *\n     * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder#append for adding records to the batch\n     * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder#build for finalizing the batch\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               int maxSize) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n            logAppendTime, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n            false, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, maxSize);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                            TimestampType timestampType, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                            int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch, boolean isTransactional,\n                                            SimpleRecord... records) {\n        if (records.length == 0)\n            return MemoryRecords.EMPTY;\n        int sizeEstimate = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytes(magic, compression.type(), Arrays.asList(records));\n public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long initialOffset, long timestamp, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                                         long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                         EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        int endTxnMarkerBatchSize = DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01LQ5EHtDgghugHx5cSewZkq","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NKyQBuWei3RfHqDPiP7NW2","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/documentation.md","content":"# Kafka Record Batch Serialization - Javadoc Summary\n\nThis document summarizes the Javadoc additions made to Kafka's record batch serialization classes in `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/`.\n\n## Overview\n\nThree core classes handle record batch serialization in Kafka:\n1. **RecordBatch** - Interface defining batch operations\n2. **DefaultRecordBatch** - Concrete implementation for magic v2+\n3. **MemoryRecords** - In-memory collection and management of batches\n\n## Class-Level Documentation Enhancements\n\n### RecordBatch (Interface)\n\n**Purpose:** Container and interface for Kafka message batches across all format versions.\n\n**Key Additions:**\n- **Thread Safety:** Explicitly documented as NOT thread-safe (requires external synchronization)\n- **Role in Protocol:** Explained fundamental importance in wire protocol and commit log format\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Lazy decompression for compressed batches (on-demand via iterators)\n  - Direct buffer access for uncompressed data (no copying)\n  - Enables idempotent producers, transactions, and log compaction features\n\n**Cross-References:**\n- {@link DefaultRecordBatch} for magic v2+ implementation\n- {@link MemoryRecords} for batch collections\n- {@link Record} for individual records\n\n### DefaultRecordBatch (Concrete Implementation)\n\n**Purpose:** Binary serialization and parsing of record batches in Kafka's v2+ format.\n\n**Key Additions:**\n- **Thread Safety:** NOT thread-safe; state can be modified in-place (e.g., CRC updates)\n- **Binary Format:** Documented full schema with field offsets and sizes\n- **CRC Computation:**\n  - Uses CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial\n  - Covers attributes field through end of batch\n  - Partition leader epoch excluded to avoid recomputation\n- **Log Compaction Semantics:** Explained preservation of first/last offset/sequence across compaction\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Header fields efficiently accessible without iteration\n  - Compressed batches use lazy decompression\n  - Record count efficiently retrievable via countOrNull()\n\n**Key Methods Enhanced:**\n- `baseTimestamp()` - Explains role in timestamp delta calculation and delete horizon\n- `recordInputStream()` - Documents buffer requirements for decompression\n- `streamingIterator()` - Performance impact of buffer allocation and reuse\n\n### MemoryRecords (In-Memory Collection)\n\n**Purpose:** ByteBuffer-backed collection and filtering of record batches.\n\n**Key Additions:**\n- **Thread Safety:** NOT thread-safe; backing buffer modified in-place, requires synchronization\n- **Use Cases:** Reading/modifying log buffers, network data, batch collections\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Lazy batch iteration (parsed incrementally)\n  - Lazy decompression (on-demand during record read)\n  - No copying of record data; direct buffer slicing\n  - Caching of validBytes() after first computation\n- **Buffer States:** Documents read-only vs. writable modes\n\n**Key Methods Enhanced:**\n- `readableRecords()` - Factory method for reading existing batches\n- `validBytes()` - Explains partial batch handling and caching behavior\n- `filterTo()` - Documents log compaction filtering, batch/record retention logic\n- `withRecords()` - Convenience factory for creating simple non-idempotent batches\n- `builder()` - Creates builders for constructing new batches\n\n## Method-Level Documentation\n\n### Critical Method: RecordBatch.streamingIterator()\n\nEnhanced with performance guidance:\n- **Memory Efficiency:** Delays decompression until records requested\n- **Buffer Allocation:** Notes that small batches allocate large buffers (64 KB for LZ4)\n- **Optimization:** Recommends reusable BufferSupplier for small batch scenarios\n- **Resource Management:** Emphasizes need to close iterator for cleanup\n\n### Critical Method: MemoryRecords.filterTo()\n\nEnhanced for log compaction use case:\n- **Filtering Process:** Step-by-step explanation of batch and record evaluation\n- **Retention Modes:** DELETE, RETAIN_EMPTY, DELETE_EMPTY semantics\n- **Performance:** Buffer overflow handling, decompression on-demand\n- **Metrics:** Explains returned FilterResult structure (bytes read/retained, max offset/timestamp)\n\n### Critical Method: MemoryRecords.validBytes()\n\nEnhanced to clarify edge cases:\n- **Partial Batches:** Explains why result may be < sizeInBytes()\n- **Caching:** Performance note about first-call iteration cost\n- **Use Cases:** When to use vs. sizeInBytes()\n\n## Documentation Best Practices Applied\n\n### Thread Safety Documentation\nEvery class explicitly states thread-safety guarantee:\n- Format: \"Thread Safety: This class is **not thread-safe**\" with explanation\n- Synchronization required at what boundary\n- Example: batch mutation during log appending\n\n### Performance Implications\nPerformance characteristics documented for methods that could be optimized:\n- Lazy decompression and iteration patterns\n- Buffer allocation costs for decompression\n- Caching behavior and amortization\n- Recommendations for BufferSupplier usage\n\n### Kafka Protocol Context\nDocumentation explains why each class exists in protocol architecture:\n- Producer ID/epoch for idempotence\n- Sequence numbers for duplicate detection\n- CRC-32C for corruption detection\n- BaseOffset/sequence preservation during compaction\n\n### Cross-References\nJavadoc uses `{@link}` tags to connect related classes:\n- RecordBatch → DefaultRecordBatch (implementation)\n- MemoryRecords → MemoryRecordsBuilder (creation)\n- DefaultRecordBatch → Record (composition)\n- filterTo() → RecordFilter (dependency)\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. **RecordBatch.java** (Interface)\n   - Enhanced class-level Javadoc\n   - Improved method documentation:\n     - isValid() / ensureValid() - CRC explanation\n     - baseOffset() - Performance implications\n     - streamingIterator() - Buffer management details\n\n2. **DefaultRecordBatch.java** (Implementation)\n   - Comprehensive class-level documentation with schema, thread-safety, CRC, compaction\n   - Enhanced method documentation:\n     - baseTimestamp() - Timestamp delta and delete horizon explanation\n     - recordInputStream() - Decompression buffer guidance\n\n3. **MemoryRecords.java** (Collection)\n   - Detailed class-level documentation with use cases, thread-safety, performance\n   - Enhanced factory methods:\n     - readableRecords() - Purpose and buffer lifetime\n     - builder() - Configuration defaults and use cases\n     - withRecords() - Convenience factory documentation\n   - Enhanced instance methods:\n     - validBytes() - Partial batch semantics and caching\n     - filterTo() - Log compaction process and metrics\n\n## Compliance with Quality Standards\n\n✓ **Thread-Safety:** Every class documents thread-safety guarantee explicitly\n✓ **Performance:** Serialization/deserialization characteristics noted with optimization guidance\n✓ **No Private Methods:** Documentation only added to public API\n✓ **Standard Tags:** Uses @param, @return, @throws, @see, {@link}\n✓ **No Logic Changes:** Implementation unchanged; documentation-only additions\n✓ **Cross-References:** Related classes and methods linked via {@link}\n\n## Key Takeaways for Users\n\n### For Log Reading\n- Use `MemoryRecords.readableRecords()` to wrap buffers for batch iteration\n- Use `streamingIterator()` with reusable BufferSupplier for memory efficiency\n- Thread-safety requires external synchronization\n\n### For Record Production\n- Use `MemoryRecordsBuilder` or `MemoryRecords.withRecords()` factory methods\n- For idempotent producers, use `withIdempotentRecords()` (includes sequence numbers)\n- For transactional producers, use `withTransactionalRecords()`\n\n### For Log Compaction\n- `filterTo()` implements retention logic; pass custom RecordFilter\n- Understand that producer state (base sequence) is preserved in empty batches\n- Performance optimized through lazy decompression\n\n### For Correctness\n- BaseOffset/LastOffset preserved during compaction\n- BaseSequence/LastSequence preserved for duplicate detection\n- BaseTimestamp preserved (or set to delete horizon) for tombstone expiration\n- CRC-32C protects batch integrity (partition leader epoch excluded)\n\n## Implementation Architecture Notes\n\nThe three classes work together in the Kafka protocol:\n\n```\nWire Protocol / Log Bytes\n         ↓\nMemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer)\n         ↓\nIterator<MutableRecordBatch> via batches()\n         ↓\nDefaultRecordBatch (parses v2+ format)\n         ↓\nIterator<Record> via streamingIterator()\n         ↓\nIndividual Record processing\n```\n\nDuring write:\n```\nProducer Records\n         ↓\nMemoryRecordsBuilder (creates batches)\n         ↓\nDefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader() / append()\n         ↓\nMemoryRecords.build() returns ByteBuffer\n         ↓\nWire Protocol / Log Bytes\n```\n\nDuring compaction:\n```\nMemoryRecords.filterTo(RecordFilter, ...)\n         ↓\nRecordFilter.checkBatchRetention() & shouldRetainRecord()\n         ↓\nFiltered batches/records written back\n         ↓\nProducer state (base sequence) preserved in empty Kafka Record Batch Serialization - Javadoc Summary\n\nThis document summarizes the Javadoc additions made to Kafka's record batch serialization classes in `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/`.\n\n## Overview\n\nThree core classes handle record batch serialization in Kafka:\n1. **RecordBatch** - Interface defining batch operations\n2. **DefaultRecordBatch** - Concrete implementation for magic v2+\n3. **MemoryRecords** - In-memory collection and management of batches\n\n## Class-Level Documentation Enhancements\n\n### RecordBatch (Interface)\n\n**Purpose:** Container and interface for Kafka message batches across all format versions.\n\n**Key Additions:**\n- **Thread Safety:** Explicitly documented as NOT thread-safe (requires external synchronization)\n- **Role in Protocol:** Explained fundamental importance in wire protocol and commit log format\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Lazy decompression for compressed batches (on-demand via iterators)\n  - Direct buffer access for uncompressed data (no copying)\n  - Enables idempotent producers, transactions, and log compaction features\n\n**Cross-References:**\n- {@link DefaultRecordBatch} for magic v2+ implementation\n- {@link MemoryRecords} for batch collections\n- {@link Record} for individual records\n\n### DefaultRecordBatch (Concrete Implementation)\n\n**Purpose:** Binary serialization and parsing of record batches in Kafka's v2+ format.\n\n**Key Additions:**\n- **Thread Safety:** NOT thread-safe; state can be modified in-place (e.g., CRC updates)\n- **Binary Format:** Documented full schema with field offsets and sizes\n- **CRC Computation:**\n  - Uses CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial\n  - Covers attributes field through end of batch\n  - Partition leader epoch excluded to avoid recomputation\n- **Log Compaction Semantics:** Explained preservation of first/last offset/sequence across compaction\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Header fields efficiently accessible without iteration\n  - Compressed batches use lazy decompression\n  - Record count efficiently retrievable via countOrNull()\n\n**Key Methods Enhanced:**\n- `baseTimestamp()` - Explains role in timestamp delta calculation and delete horizon\n- `recordInputStream()` - Documents buffer requirements for decompression\n- `streamingIterator()` - Performance impact of buffer allocation and reuse\n\n### MemoryRecords (In-Memory Collection)\n\n**Purpose:** ByteBuffer-backed collection and filtering of record batches.\n\n**Key Additions:**\n- **Thread Safety:** NOT thread-safe; backing buffer modified in-place, requires synchronization\n- **Use Cases:** Reading/modifying log buffers, network data, batch collections\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Lazy batch iteration (parsed incrementally)\n  - Lazy decompression (on-demand during record read)\n  - No copying of record data; direct buffer slicing\n  - Caching of validBytes() after first computation\n- **Buffer States:** Documents read-only vs. writable modes\n\n**Key Methods Enhanced:**\n- `readableRecords()` - Factory method for reading existing batches\n- `validBytes()` - Explains partial batch handling and caching behavior\n- `filterTo()` - Documents log compaction filtering, batch/record retention logic\n- `withRecords()` - Convenience factory for creating simple non-idempotent batches\n- `builder()` - Creates builders for constructing new batches\n\n## Method-Level Documentation\n\n### Critical Method: RecordBatch.streamingIterator()\n\nEnhanced with performance guidance:\n- **Memory Efficiency:** Delays decompression until records requested\n- **Buffer Allocation:** Notes that small batches allocate large buffers (64 KB for LZ4)\n- **Optimization:** Recommends reusable BufferSupplier for small batch scenarios\n- **Resource Management:** Emphasizes need to close iterator for cleanup\n\n### Critical Method: MemoryRecords.filterTo()\n\nEnhanced for log compaction use case:\n- **Filtering Process:** Step-by-step explanation of batch and record evaluation\n- **Retention Modes:** DELETE, RETAIN_EMPTY, DELETE_EMPTY semantics\n- **Performance:** Buffer overflow handling, decompression on-demand\n- **Metrics:** Explains returned FilterResult structure (bytes read/retained, max offset/timestamp)\n\n### Critical Method: MemoryRecords.validBytes()\n\nEnhanced to clarify edge cases:\n- **Partial Batches:** Explains why result may be < sizeInBytes()\n- **Caching:** Performance note about first-call iteration cost\n- **Use Cases:** When to use vs. sizeInBytes()\n\n## Documentation Best Practices Applied\n\n### Thread Safety Documentation\nEvery class explicitly states thread-safety guarantee:\n- Format: \"Thread Safety: This class is **not thread-safe**\" with explanation\n- Synchronization required at what boundary\n- Example: batch mutation during log appending\n\n### Performance Implications\nPerformance characteristics documented for methods that could be optimized:\n- Lazy decompression and iteration patterns\n- Buffer allocation costs for decompression\n- Caching behavior and amortization\n- Recommendations for BufferSupplier usage\n\n### Kafka Protocol Context\nDocumentation explains why each class exists in protocol architecture:\n- Producer ID/epoch for idempotence\n- Sequence numbers for duplicate detection\n- CRC-32C for corruption detection\n- BaseOffset/sequence preservation during compaction\n\n### Cross-References\nJavadoc uses `{@link}` tags to connect related classes:\n- RecordBatch → DefaultRecordBatch (implementation)\n- MemoryRecords → MemoryRecordsBuilder (creation)\n- DefaultRecordBatch → Record (composition)\n- filterTo() → RecordFilter (dependency)\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. **RecordBatch.java** (Interface)\n   - Enhanced class-level Javadoc\n   - Improved method documentation:\n     - isValid() / ensureValid() - CRC explanation\n     - baseOffset() - Performance implications\n     - streamingIterator() - Buffer management details\n\n2. **DefaultRecordBatch.java** horizon explanation\n     - recordInputStream() - Decompression buffer guidance\n\n3. **MemoryRecords.java** (Collection)\n   - Detailed class-level documentation with use cases, thread-safety, performance\n   - Enhanced factory methods:\n     - readableRecords() - Purpose and buffer lifetime\n     - builder() - Configuration defaults and use cases\n     - withRecords() - Convenience factory additions\n✓ **Cross-References:** Related classes and methods linked via {@link}\n\n## Key Takeaways for Users\n\n### For Log Reading\n- Use `MemoryRecords.readableRecords()` to wrap buffers for batch iteration\n- Use `streamingIterator()` with reusable BufferSupplier for memory efficiency\n- Thread-safety requires external synchronization\n\n### For Record Production\n- Use `MemoryRecordsBuilder` or `MemoryRecords.withRecords()` factory methods\n- For idempotent producers, use `withIdempotentRecords()` (includes sequence numbers)\n- For transactional producers, use `withTransactionalRecords()`\n\n### For Log Compaction\n- `filterTo()` implements retention logic; pass custom RecordFilter\n- Understand that producer state (base sequence) is preserved in empty batches\n- Performance optimized through lazy decompression\n\n### For Correctness\n- BaseOffset/LastOffset preserved during compaction\n- BaseSequence/LastSequence preserved for duplicate detection\n- BaseTimestamp preserved (or set to delete horizon) for tombstone expiration\n- CRC-32C protects batch integrity (partition leader epoch excluded)\n\n## Implementation Architecture Notes\n\nThe three classes work together in the Kafka protocol:\n\n```\nWire Protocol / Log Bytes\n         ↓\nMemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer)\n         ↓\nIterator<MutableRecordBatch> via batches()\n         ↓\nDefaultRecordBatch (parses v2+ format)\n         ↓\nIterator<Record> via streamingIterator()\n         ↓\nIndividual Record processing\n```\n\nDuring write:\n```\nProducer Records\n         ↓\nMemoryRecordsBuilder (creates batches)\n         ↓\nDefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader() / append()\n         ↓\nMemoryRecords.build() returns ByteBuffer\n         ↓\nWire Protocol / Log Bytes\n```\n\nDuring compaction:\n```\nMemoryRecords.filterTo(RecordFilter, ...)\n         ↓\nRecordFilter.checkBatchRetention() & shouldRetainRecord()\n         ↓\nFiltered batches/records written back\n         ↓\nProducer state (base sequence) preserved in empty batches\n```\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

The backing ByteBuffer and batch\n * objects can be modified in-place (e.g., CRC updates during log appending). External synchronization\n * is required for concurrent access. However, individual batch iteration is safe if the buffer contents\n * are not modified during iteration.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b>\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Batch iteration is lazy—batches are parsed incrementally, not all at once.</li>\n *   <li>Record decompression is lazy—only decompressed when actually read via iterators.</li>\n *   <li>No copying of record data; all reads directly reference the backing buffer via slices.</li>\n *   <li>{@link #validBytes()} requires scanning all batches; the result is cached.</li>\n * </ul></p>\n *\n * <p><b>Buffer Management:</b> The ByteBuffer backing this instance can be in several states:\n * <ul>\n *   <li><b>Read-only:</b> Created via {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)}, position/limit managed externally.</li>\n *   <li><b>Writable:</b> Created via {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder#build()}, position and limit reflect valid data.</li>\n * </ul></p>\n *\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for creating this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n     * @param channel The channel to write to\n     * @return The number of bytes written\n     * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * The total number of bytes in this record collection that form complete, valid state can be modified in-place (e.g., CRC updates)\n    37→- **Binary Format:** Documented full schema with field offsets and sizes\n    38→- **CRC Computation:**\n    39→  - Uses CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial\n    40→  - Covers attributes field through end of batch\n    41→  - Partition leader epoch excluded to avoid recomputation\n    42→- **Log Compaction Semantics:** Explained preservation of first/last offset/sequence across compaction\n    43→- **Performance Characteristics:**\n    44→  - Header fields efficiently accessible without iteration\n    45→  - Compressed batches use lazy decompression\n    46→  - Record count efficiently retrievable via countOrNull()\n    47→\n    48→**Key Methods Enhanced:**\n    49→- `baseTimestamp()` - Explains role in timestamp delta calculation and delete horizon\n    50→- `recordInputStream()` - Documents buffer requirements for decompression\n    51→- `streamingIterator()` - Performance impact of buffer allocation and reuse\n    52→\n    53→### MemoryRecords (In-Memory Collection)\n    54→\n    55→**Purpose:** ByteBuffer-backed collection and filtering of record batches.\n    56→\n    57→**Key Additions:**\n    58→- **Thread Safety:** NOT thread-safe; backing buffer modified in-place, requires synchronization\n    59→- **Use Cases:** Reading/modifying log buffers, network data, batch collections\n    60→- **Performance Characteristics:**\n    61→  - Lazy batch iteration (parsed incrementally)\n    62→  - Lazy decompression (on-demand during record read)\n    63→  - No copying of record data; direct buffer slicing\n    64→  - Caching of validBytes() after first computation\n    65→- **Buffer States:** Documents read-only vs. writable modes\n    66→\n    67→**Key Methods Enhanced:**\n    68→- `readableRecords()` - Factory method for reading existing batches\n    69→- `validBytes()` - Explains partial batch handling and caching behavior\n    70→- `filterTo()` - Documents log compaction filtering, batch/record retention logic\n    71→- `withRecords()` - Convenience factory for creating simple non-idempotent batches\n    72→- `builder()` - Creates builders for constructing new batches\n    73→\n    74→## Method-Level Documentation\n    75→\n    76→### Critical Method: RecordBatch.streamingIterator()\n    77→\n    78→Enhanced with performance guidance:\n    79→- **Memory Efficiency:** Delays decompression until records requested\n    80→- **Buffer Allocation:** Notes that small batches allocate large buffers (64 KB for LZ4)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. state can be modified in-place (e.g., CRC updates)\n- **Binary Format:** Documented full schema with field offsets and sizes\n- **CRC Computation:**\n  - Uses CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial\n  - Covers attributes field through end of batch\n  - Partition leader epoch excluded to avoid recomputation\n- **Log Compaction Semantics:** Explained preservation of first/last offset/sequence across compaction\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Header fields efficiently accessible without iteration\n  - Compressed batches use lazy decompression\n  - Record count efficiently retrievable via countOrNull()\n\n**Key Methods Enhanced:**\n- `baseTimestamp()` - Explains role in timestamp delta calculation and delete horizon\n- `recordInputStream()` - Documents buffer requirements for decompression\n- `streamingIterator()` - Performance impact of buffer allocation and reuse\n\n### MemoryRecords (In-Memory Collection)\n\n**Purpose:** ByteBuffer-backed collection and filtering of record batches.\n\n**Key Additions:**\n- **Thread Safety:** NOT thread-safe; backing buffer modified in-place, requires synchronization\n- **Use Cases:** Reading/modifying log buffers, network data, batch collections\n- **Performance Characteristics:**\n  - Lazy batch iteration (parsed incrementally)\n  - Lazy decompression (on-demand during record read)\n  - No copying of record data; direct buffer slicing\n  - Caching of validBytes() after first computation\n- **Buffer States:** Documents read-only vs. writable modes\n\n**Key Methods Enhanced:**\n- `readableRecords()` - Factory method for reading existing batches\n- `validBytes()` - Explains partial batch handling and caching behavior\n- `filterTo()` - Documents log compaction filtering, batch/record retention logic\n- `withRecords()` - Convenience factory for creating simple non-idempotent batches\n- `builder()` - Creates builders for constructing new batches\n\n## Method-Level Documentation\n\n### Critical Method: RecordBatch.streamingIterator()\n\nEnhanced with performance guidance:\n- **Memory Efficiency:** Delays decompression until records requested\n- **Buffer Allocation:** Notes that small batches allocate large buffers (64 KB for LZ4)","numLines":80,"startLine":1,"totalLines":226}}}
